title: Schema-contradiction fixture: UI fields on a non-UI item
summary: A structurally valid binder whose single work item is a backend data migration yet carries component_map, icon_map, and token_changes. karta-plan SKILL.md promises 'Emitting them on a migration, a CLI command, or a data pipeline item is a schema error — the validator will catch it', but both binder-schema.json (they are optional array properties on every work item) and validate_binder.py accept it. Both validators accept exactly what the promise claims is caught.
motivation: Pin the false safety net: the plan prose claims a validator catches UI keys on non-UI items, and neither validator does.

work_items.title: Migrate the accounts table to the new partition key
work_items.summary: A pure backend data migration with no UI surface, yet it carries UI-only keys.
